actions-list.actionItem: The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

The House received a message from the Clerk. Pursuant to the permission granted in Clause 2(h) of Rule II of the Rules of the U.S. House of Representatives, the Clerk notified the House that he had received the following message from the Secretary of the Senate on April 17, 2026, at 11:15 a.m.: That the Senate passed H.R. 8322.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches.
The Chair designated Mr. Ezell to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by Pastor Chad Weeks, Russell Baptist Church, Green Cove Springs, Florida.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of April 20.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 2:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 2:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Randy K. Weber Sr. to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
